lines.gurmukhi: ਬੁਢਾ ਹੋਆ ਸੇਖ ਫਰੀਦੁ ਕੰਬਣਿ ਲਗੀ ਦੇਹ ॥
lines.translit: budtaa hoaa sekh fareed kanban lagee deh |
lines.english: Shaykh Fareed has grown old, and his body has begun to tremble.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਬਾਰਿ ਪਰਾਇਐ ਬੈਸਣਾ ਸਾਂਈ ਮੁਝੈ ਨ ਦੇਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa baar paraaeiai baisanaa saanee mujhai na dehi |
lines.english: Fareed begs, O Lord, do not make me sit at another's door.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਸਉ ਵਰੑਿਆ ਜੀਵਣਾ ਭੀ ਤਨੁ ਹੋਸੀ ਖੇਹ ॥੪੧॥
lines.translit: je sau varaiaa jeevanaa bhee tan hosee kheh |41|
lines.english: Even if he could live for hundreds of years, his body will eventually turn to dust. ||41||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੰਧਿ ਕੁਹਾੜਾ ਸਿਰਿ ਘੜਾ ਵਣਿ ਕੈ ਸਰੁ ਲੋਹਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: kandh kuhaarraa sir gharraa van kai sar lohaar |
lines.english: With the axe on his shoulder, and a bucket on his head, the blacksmith is ready to cut down the tree.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਤੂ ਏਵੈ ਰਖਸੀ ਜੀਉ ਸਰੀਰਹੁ ਲੇਹਿ ॥੪੨॥
lines.translit: je too evai rakhasee jeeo sareerahu lehi |42|
lines.english: If this is the way you are going to keep me, then go ahead and take the life out of my body. ||42||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਹਉ ਲੋੜੀ ਸਹੁ ਆਪਣਾ ਤੂ ਲੋੜਹਿ ਅੰਗਿਆਰ ॥੪੩॥
lines.translit: fareedaa hau lorree sahu aapanaa too lorreh angiaar |43|
lines.english: Fareed, I long for my Lord; you long only for the charcoal. ||43||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਇਕਨਾ ਆਟਾ ਅਗਲਾ ਇਕਨਾ ਨਾਹੀ ਲੋਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa ikanaa aattaa agalaa ikanaa naahee lon |
lines.english: Fareed, some have lots of flour, while others do not even have salt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗੈ ਗਏ ਸਿੰਞਾਪਸਨਿ ਚੋਟਾਂ ਖਾਸੀ ਕਉਣੁ ॥੪੪॥
lines.translit: agai ge sinyaapasan chottaan khaasee kaun |44|
lines.english: When they go beyond this world, it shall be seen, who will be punished. ||44||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਸਿ ਦਮਾਮੇ ਛਤੁ ਸਿਰਿ ਭੇਰੀ ਸਡੋ ਰਡ ॥
lines.translit: paas damaame chhat sir bheree saddo radd |
lines.english: Drums were beaten in their honor, there were canopies above their heads, and bugles announced their coming.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਇ ਸੁਤੇ ਜੀਰਾਣ ਮਹਿ ਥੀਏ ਅਤੀਮਾ ਗਡ ॥੪੫॥
lines.translit: jaae sute jeeraan meh thee ateemaa gadd |45|
lines.english: They have gone to sleep in the cemetary, buried like poor orphans. ||45||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕੋਠੇ ਮੰਡਪ ਮਾੜੀਆ ਉਸਾਰੇਦੇ ਭੀ ਗਏ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kotthe manddap maarreea usaarede bhee ge |
lines.english: Fareed, those who built houses, mansions and lofty buildings, are also gone.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੂੜਾ ਸਉਦਾ ਕਰਿ ਗਏ ਗੋਰੀ ਆਇ ਪਏ ॥੪੬॥
lines.translit: koorraa saudaa kar ge goree aae pe |46|
lines.english: They made false deals, and were dropped into their graves. ||46||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਖਿੰਥੜਿ ਮੇਖਾ ਅਗਲੀਆ ਜਿੰਦੁ ਨ ਕਾਈ ਮੇਖ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa khintharr mekhaa agaleea jind na kaaee mekh |
lines.english: Fareed, there are many seams on the patched coat, but there are no seams on the soul.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਦੁਹੁ ਦੀਵੀ ਬਲੰਦਿਆ ਮਲਕੁ ਬਹਿਠਾ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa duhu deevee balandiaa malak bahitthaa aae |
lines.english: Fareed, the two lamps are lit, but death has come anyway.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਾਰੀ ਆਪੋ ਆਪਣੀ ਚਲੇ ਮਸਾਇਕ ਸੇਖ ॥੪੭॥
lines.translit: vaaree aapo aapanee chale masaaeik sekh |47|
lines.english: The shaykhs and their disciples have all departed, each in his own turn. ||47||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਵੇਖੁ ਕਪਾਹੈ ਜਿ ਥੀਆ ਜਿ ਸਿਰਿ ਥੀਆ ਤਿਲਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa vekh kapaahai ji theea ji sir theea tilaah |
lines.english: Fareed, look at what has happened to the cotton and the sesame seed,

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੜੁ ਲੀਤਾ ਘਟੁ ਲੁਟਿਆ ਦੀਵੜੇ ਗਇਆ ਬੁਝਾਇ ॥੪੮॥
lines.translit: garr leetaa ghatt luttiaa deevarre geaa bujhaae |48|
lines.english: It has captured the fortress of the body, and plundered the home of the heart; it extinguishes the lamps and departs. ||48||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਮਾਦੈ ਅਰੁ ਕਾਗਦੈ ਕੁੰਨੇ ਕੋਇਲਿਆਹ ॥
lines.translit: kamaadai ar kaagadai kune koeiliaah |
lines.english: the sugar cane and paper, the clay pots and the charcoal.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੰਦੇ ਅਮਲ ਕਰੇਦਿਆ ਏਹ ਸਜਾਇ ਤਿਨਾਹ ॥੪੯॥
lines.translit: mande amal karediaa eeh sajaae tinaah |49|
lines.english: This is the punishment for those who do evil deeds. ||49||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕੰਨਿ ਮੁਸਲਾ ਸੂਫੁ ਗਲਿ ਦਿਲਿ ਕਾਤੀ ਗੁੜੁ ਵਾਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kan musalaa soof gal dil kaatee gurr vaat |
lines.english: Fareed, you wear your prayer shawl on your shoulders and the robes of a Sufi; your words are sweet, but there is a dagger in your heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਹਰਿ ਦਿਸੈ ਚਾਨਣਾ ਦਿਲਿ ਅੰਧਿਆਰੀ ਰਾਤਿ ॥੫੦॥
lines.translit: baahar disai chaananaa dil andhiaaree raat |50|
lines.english: Outwardly, you look bright, but your heart is dark as night. ||50||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤਨ ਰਤੇ ਰਬ ਸਿਉ ਤਿਨ ਤਨਿ ਰਤੁ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥੫੧॥
lines.translit: jo tan rate rab siau tin tan rat na hoe |51|
lines.english: Those bodies which are imbued with the Lord - those bodies contain no blood. ||51||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਰਤੀ ਰਤੁ ਨ ਨਿਕਲੈ ਜੇ ਤਨੁ ਚੀਰੈ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa ratee rat na nikalai je tan cheerai koe |
lines.english: Fareed, not even a drop of blood would issue forth, if someone cut my body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਸਹ ਰਤੇ ਆਪਣੇ ਤਿਤੁ ਤਨਿ ਲੋਭੁ ਰਤੁ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: jo seh rate aapane tith tan lobh rat na hoe |
lines.english: Those who are imbued with their Lord, do not have the blood of greed in their bodies.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਤਨੁ ਸਭੋ ਰਤੁ ਹੈ ਰਤੁ ਬਿਨੁ ਤੰਨੁ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: eihu tan sabho rat hai rat bin tan na hoe |
lines.english: This body is all blood; without blood, this body could not exist.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਬੈਸੰਤਰਿ ਧਾਤੁ ਸੁਧੁ ਹੋਇ ਤਿਉ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਭਉ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਮੈਲੁ ਗਵਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jiau baisantar dhaat sudh hoe tiau har kaa bhau duramat mail gavaae |
lines.english: Just as metal is purified by fire, the Fear of God removes the filthy residues of evil-mindedness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈ ਪਇਐ ਤਨੁ ਖੀਣੁ ਹੋਇ ਲੋਭੁ ਰਤੁ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: bhai peiai tan kheen hoe lobh rat vichahu jaae |
lines.english: When the Fear of God fills the body, it becomes thin; the blood of greed departs from within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਸੋਈ ਸਰਵਰੁ ਢੂਢਿ ਲਹੁ ਜਿਥਹੁ ਲਭੀ ਵਥੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa soee saravar dtoodt lahu jithahu labhee vath |
lines.english: Fareed, seek that sacred pool, in which the genuine article is found.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤੇ ਜਨ ਸੋਹਣੇ ਜਿ ਰਤੇ ਹਰਿ ਰੰਗੁ ਲਾਇ ॥੫੨॥
lines.translit: naanak te jan sohane ji rate har rang laae |52|
lines.english: O Nanak, those humble beings are beautiful, who are imbued with the Lord's Love. ||52||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਨੰਢੀ ਕੰਤੁ ਨ ਰਾਵਿਓ ਵਡੀ ਥੀ ਮੁਈਆਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa nandtee kant na raavio vaddee thee mueeaas |
lines.english: Fareed, when she is young, she does not enjoy her Husband. When she grows up, she dies.

lines.gurmukhi: ਛਪੜਿ ਢੂਢੈ ਕਿਆ ਹੋਵੈ ਚਿਕੜਿ ਡੁਬੈ ਹਥੁ ॥੫੩॥
lines.translit: chhaparr dtoodtai kiaa hovai chikarr ddubai hath |53|
lines.english: Why do you bother to search in the pond? Your hand will only sink into the mud. ||53||

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨ ਕੂਕੇਂਦੀ ਗੋਰ ਮੇਂ ਤੈ ਸਹ ਨਾ ਮਿਲੀਆਸੁ ॥੫੪॥
lines.translit: dhan kookendee gor men tai seh naa mileeaas |54|
lines.english: Lying in the grave, the soul-bride cries, "I did not meet You, my Lord." ||54||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਸਿਰੁ ਪਲਿਆ ਦਾੜੀ ਪਲੀ ਮੁਛਾਂ ਭੀ ਪਲੀਆਂ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa sir paliaa daarree palee muchhaan bhee paleean |
lines.english: Fareed, your hair has turned grey, your beard has turned grey, and your moustache has turned grey.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੇ ਮਨ ਗਹਿਲੇ ਬਾਵਲੇ ਮਾਣਹਿ ਕਿਆ ਰਲੀਆਂ ॥੫੫॥
lines.translit: re man gahile baavale maaneh kiaa raleean |55|
lines.english: O my thoughtless and insane mind, why are you indulging in pleasures? ||55||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕੋਠੇ ਧੁਕਣੁ ਕੇਤੜਾ ਪਿਰ ਨੀਦੜੀ ਨਿਵਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kotthe dhukan ketarraa pir needarree nivaar |
lines.english: Fareed, how long can you run on the rooftop? You are asleep to your Husband Lord - give it up!

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਦਿਹ ਲਧੇ ਗਾਣਵੇ ਗਏ ਵਿਲਾੜਿ ਵਿਲਾੜਿ ॥੫੬॥
lines.translit: jo dih ladhe gaanave ge vilaarr vilaarr |56|
lines.english: The days which were allotted to you are numbered, and they are passing, passing away. ||56||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕੋਠੇ ਮੰਡਪ ਮਾੜੀਆ ਏਤੁ ਨ ਲਾਏ ਚਿਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kotthe manddap maarreea et na laae chit |
lines.english: Fareed, houses, mansions and balconies - do not attach your consciousness to these.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਟੀ ਪਈ ਅਤੋਲਵੀ ਕੋਇ ਨ ਹੋਸੀ ਮਿਤੁ ॥੫੭॥
lines.translit: mittee pee atolavee koe na hosee mit |57|
lines.english: When these collapse into heaps of dust, none of them will be your friend. ||57||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਮੰਡਪ ਮਾਲੁ ਨ ਲਾਇ ਮਰਗ ਸਤਾਣੀ ਚਿਤਿ ਧਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa manddap maal na laae marag sataanee chit dhar |
lines.english: Fareed, do not focus on mansions and wealth; center your consciousness on death, your powerful enemy.
